top_categories.workout_context: 6,604ft of climbing means repeated surges above threshold. VO2max work is how you survive the fifth climb, not just the first.
Race-pace efforts that mimic GFNY Cannes' demands. Pacing, fueling, and tactical decisions under fatigue. Practice the race before April.
68 miles on mixed terrain burns energy faster than the distance suggests. Train the fade resistance now.
GFNY Cannes' mixed terrain forces constant power changes. Surge over the rough stuff, settle on the smooth, repeat for 68 miles.
97ft/mi means long efforts at or near FTP on every climb. If your threshold cracks at minute 15, you'll walk the rest.
88–92% FTP is where you'll spend the hardest sustained miles of GFNY Cannes. Train it until it feels boring.
Endurance with intensity spikes baked in. Exactly what 6,604ft of climbing on mixed terrain does to your power file on race day.
Short, max efforts are unavoidable. Punchy climbs, gap closures, attacks on mixed terrain. If you haven't trained them, you'll crack.
Base fitness for GFNY Cannes. Without this, the hard workouts break you down instead of building you up.
At GFNY Cannes, surges push you above threshold and you have to recover while still riding. Over-unders train exactly that.
Double-threshold training builds sustained power with less fatigue. More FTP per hour of training.
How long can you hold 105% FTP before you blow? At GFNY Cannes, that number decides whether you bridge or get dropped.

race_overlay.nutrition: Target 40–60g carbs/hour for GFNY Cannes’s 68 miles. Front-load calories in the first half. One bottle per hour minimum, more in heat.
race_overlay.altitude: Moderate altitude at GFNY Cannes. Arrive 2–3 days early. Reduce intensity expectations by 5–10%. Hydrate aggressively.

pack_summary: This 10-workout pack focuses on VO2max, Race Simulation, and Durability to prepare you for 68 miles of mixed terrain in Cannes, French Riviera, France.
